title: SoundHound AI Analysts Cut Price Targets Despite Record Q1 Revenue Beat
text: Following SoundHound AI's Q1 2026 earnings announcement featuring record revenue of $44.2 million and 52% year-over-year growth, several analysts trimmed their price targets despite the strong financial performance. DA Davidson analyst Gil Luria lowered his price target from $14 to $12, and Northland's Michael Latimore also cut his target from $14 to $12. However, analysts at Wedbush and Cantor Fitzgerald reiterated their Buy ratings with price targets of $12 and $15 respectively. The divergence in analyst sentiment reflects concerns about the company's path to profitability despite impressive revenue growth. Core automotive and IoT AI revenue grew 88% organically, demonstrating robust demand across the company's key business segments. Management's guidance for 2026 revenue of $225-260 million and 2027 revenue of $350-400 million (including LivePerson) suggests continued strong growth prospects.
